'''Luptoom''' was an [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] situated in the [[Seswenna sector/Legends|Seswenna sector]]. The planet's [[Human/Legends|Human]] population, known as [[Luptoomian/Legends|Luptoomians]], was known for its unusual style of [[Clothing/Legends|clothing]]. During the [[Separatist Crisis/Legends|Separatist Crisis]], the Luptoomian [[Nar Hida/Legends|Nar Hida]] opened a fashion [[Merchant/Legends|store]] on the planet [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]].

Following the closure of his unsuccessful business, Hida and his family attempted to return [[Homeworld/Legends|home]] to Luptoom aboard the [[AA-9 Coruscant freighter/Legends|freighter]] [[Jendirian Valley/Legends|''Jendirian Valley'']]. They succeeded only due to a chance encounter with [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] and [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Amidala]], who directed them to the assistance of the [[Refugee Relief Movement/Legends|Refugee Relief Movement]].

==Description==
Luptoom was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="CSWE Luptoomian" /> located in the [[Luptoom system/Legends|Luptoom system]], a part of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']] [[Seswenna sector/Legends|Seswenna sector]].<ref name="EAOC" /> The planet shared its name with [[Luptoomian dressing]], a [[Interstellar garden salad|salad]] [[Sauce/Legends|sauce]] served at [[Dex's Diner/Legends|Dex's Diner]] on<ref name="Dex">{{Hyperspace|url=fans/hyperspace/source/dexdiner/index.html|text=Dining at Dex's|int=Dining at Dex's}}</ref> the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] planet<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]].<ref name="Dex" />

==History==
[[File:Nar_Hida_tall.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Nar Hida, a native of Luptoom]]
By the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Separatist Crisis/Legends|Separatist Crisis]], the [[Luptoomian/Legends|Luptoomian]] [[Nar Hida/Legends|Nar Hida]], along with his [[Nar Hida's wife|wife]] and [[Nar Hida's daughter|daughter]], left his [[Homeworld/Legends|home]] on Luptoom and traveled to Coruscant. There, Hida opened a Luptoomian fashion [[Merchant/Legends|boutique]]; however, he was soon forced to close the shop due to poor sales.<ref name="DB Hida">{{DB|character|narhida|Hida, Nar}}</ref>

Boarding the [[AA-9 Coruscant freighter/Legends|freighter]] [[Jendirian Valley/Legends|''Jendirian Valley'']]<ref name="DB Hida" /> in [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]]<ref name="Date">The events described in {{DB|character|narhida|Hida, Nar}} take place concurrently with those of [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Star Wars'': Episode II ''Attack of the Clones'']], which ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates to [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]].</ref> with the intent of returning to Luptoom, the nearly [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|creditless]] Hida family learned that the [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] would only travel halfway to the planet, which threatened to leave the Luptoomians stranded away from home. Following a chance encounter aboard the [[Starship/Legends|ship]] with<ref name="DB Hida" /> [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] and [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Amidala]]<ref name="CSWE Nar Hida">{{CSWECite|II|39|Hida, Nar}}</ref>—a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] and a [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] traveling incognito<ref name="Episode II">{{Film|II}}</ref>—Hida was directed by the latter to contact the [[Refugee Relief Movement/Legends|Refugee Relief Movement]] upon the ''Jendirian Valley''{{'s}} stop at<ref name="DB Hida" /> the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]] planet<ref name="TEA" /> [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]]. The movement then helped the failed entrepreneur and his family return to Luptoom.<ref name="DB Hida" />

==Inhabitants==
Luptoom was home to a population of [[Human/Legends|Humans]] known as Luptoomians, who were known for their unusual [[Clothing/Legends|clothing]] style.<ref name="CSWE Luptoomian" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|There's no story behind the name of the Hidas' homeworld, Luptoom. It's just a goofy-sounded name I dreamed up while writing. I'd like to think that Luptoom is located somewhere near Almak, if for no other reason than it might then be the home of Luptoomian Leffingites.|Gregory Walker|<ref name="Blog">{{Blog|narbflick|9|Nar Hida: The Story Behind the Story}}</ref>}}
Luptoom was developed as part of the backstory for Nar Hida,<ref name="DB Hida" /> an originally unnamed character appearing in the [[2002]] [[prequel trilogy]] film, [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Star Wars'': Episode II ''Attack of the Clones'']].<ref name="Episode II" /> [[Gregory Walker]], writing as "NarbFlick,"<ref name="Dex" /> submitted an entry for Hida<ref name="DB Hida" /> through the [[StarWars.com]] [[Hyperspace: The Official Star Wars Fan Club|Hyperspace]] feature "[[What's The Story?]]," and it was added to the site's [[Databank (original)|Databank]] on [[September 28]], [[2006]].<ref name="What's The Story">{{SWArchive|url=databank/updates/news20060928.html|text=What's the Story, Round 7}}</ref>

Walker later claimed that there was "no story behind" Luptoom's "goofy-sounded" name and that they had "dreamed" it up while writing Hida's entry. The author also expressed their intention for Luptoom to be located in the vicinity of the world [[Almak/Legends|Almak]],<ref name="Blog" /> which had also been created by Walker via "What's The Story?".<ref name="DB Magaloof">{{DB|character|magaloof|Magaloof}}</ref> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Luptoom [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the planet Luptoom, within grid square M-18, while placing the [[Almak system/Legends|Almak system]] in the Mid Rim, grid square J-17.<ref name="TEA" />
